test.template: Run the test suite. Detect if Go (go test ./...) or TypeScript (npm/pnpm/yarn/bun test) and run accordingly. Show failures clearly.

build.template: Build the project. Detect if Go (go build ./...) or TypeScript (npm/pnpm/yarn/bun run build) and run accordingly. Report any errors.

lint.template: Run the linter. For Go use 'golangci-lint run' or 'go vet ./...'. For TypeScript use the project's configured linter (eslint, biome, etc). Report all issues.

migrate.template: Run database migrations. Check for common migration tools in order: golang-migrate, goose, sqlc, prisma, typeorm, drizzle, sequelize. If found, run the appropriate migrate command. If uncertain, ask which tool to use.

cover.template: Run tests with coverage. For Go use 'go test -coverprofile=coverage.out ./... && go tool cover -func coverage.out'. For TypeScript use the project's coverage command (vitest --coverage, jest --coverage, etc). Report coverage percentages per package.

go.description: Go backend expert — idioms, concurrency, stdlib, project layout
go.prompt: You are a senior Go backend engineer. Follow Go idioms, use proper error wrapping, leverage stdlib before third-party libs. Prefer explicit interfaces, table-driven tests, and clean package boundaries. Use gofmt style. Structure projects per standard cmd/internal/pkg layout.

ts.description: TypeScript backend expert — strict types, async patterns, Node/Deno/Bun
ts.prompt: You are a senior TypeScript backend engineer. Use strict typing, prefer interfaces over types for public APIs, handle async errors properly. Use modern ES2022+ patterns. Follow project's existing framework conventions (Express, NestJS, Hono, Elysia, etc). Structure imports clearly.
